title: Espeletiopsis sclerophylla
taxonomy: Espeletiopsis sclerophylla is a member of the plant family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Espeletiopsis, which is a group of flowering plants. More specifically, it is classified as a species within the tribe Gnaphalieae. Espeletiopsis sclerophylla is a flowering plant native to South America. Its classification is further broken down into kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Asterales.

intro: Espeletiopsis sclerophylla, also known as 'sclerophyllous espeletia,' is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and reduce oxidative stress in the body. The bioactive constituents of Espeletiopsis sclerophylla include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Espeletiopsis sclerophylla is typically prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
